def: A firm belief that someone or something is reliable and honest.
The ability to stay calm while waiting or facing difficulty.
The power to do work or be active; also electricity and fuel.
A link that joins two things, people, or places.
A signed agreement setting out the terms of a person's job.
A separate section of a company or organisation.
A discussion aimed at reaching an agreement.
The written papers needed for a task or application.
How heavy something is; also its importance.
The care given to cure an illness, or the way someone is dealt with.
A choice made after thinking about the options.
Achieving the result you wanted; doing well.
Having the duty to deal with or take care of something.
Having a strong wish to succeed or achieve a lot.
Needing to be dealt with at once.
At the same time as something else happens.
Immediately after something happens.
A personal view or belief that may not be a fact.
A feeling of not being sure about something.
A plan or aim to do something.
The regular pay an employee receives, usually each month.
A good result achieved after effort.
Careful study done to discover new facts.
The system of making, trading, and using money and goods.
The reason for which something is done or made.
Something you do regularly, often without thinking.
An exam you must pass to get a driving licence.
An unexpected event that causes damage or injury.
The city where a country's government is based.
Able to use, or written in, several languages.
A person who performs in plays or films.
A formal talk given to students at a university.
To take an active part in something you care about.
The joining back together of a country that was divided.
A structure built to remember a person or event.
A person who lived through and can describe a historical event.
To leave your own country to live permanently in another.
A sad longing for home when you are away.
To get used to a new place and feel at home there.
Unexpected and causing wonder.
A system in which the people choose their government by voting.
The way in which a word or language is spoken.
A signal with red, amber, and green lights that controls traffic.
The wheel a driver turns to steer a car.
To take place, especially of an event.
The group of elected people who make a country's laws.
Based on the idea that people rule through voting.
The point at which a course of study begins.
The act of focusing on one particular field or skill.
Useful and important for real, practical work.
A chance to move forward in your working life.
The act of working together toward a shared goal.
A distinct part of an economy or area of activity.
A very large company that operates around the world.
All the people who live in a place.
A clear understanding of what something is really like.
Something kept hidden from others.
A group of connected people, computers, or things.
The quality of having many skills or uses.
Relating to cooking and food.
Existing in large numbers; many.
The work of collecting and reporting news.
A planned course of vocational training.
A building where plays are performed.
A strong attraction or deep interest in something.
Great pleasure taken in something, often food or an experience.
To do something one after another in turn.
Being the only one of its kind; unlike anything else.
So special that it cannot be forgotten.
The person who sends a letter or parcel.
A small printed label stuck on mail to show postage is paid.
A local office where you send mail and parcels.
A service that shows where a parcel is on its way.
A formal request to search for a lost letter or parcel.
A course you take over the internet.
The right to act, speak, and think as you choose.
The right to make your own decisions about your life.
Neat and well arranged; also proper.
Feeling hopeless and ready to try anything.
Existing or happening all over the world.
The act of quitting a university course before finishing.
Official permission to enter a course, place, or profession.
To officially register as a student or member.
A chance to move up to a higher position at work.
The money a person regularly receives from work or other sources.
To take part in an activity together with others.
To ask for information about something.
Worth a lot of money, or very useful and important.
A person who gives help or assistance.
Paid work that supports people with social or personal problems.
Done by choice, without being forced or paid.
A farm bird kept for its eggs and meat.
The work of growing crops and raising farm animals.
A farm animal kept for its wool and meat.
A field of grass and wild flowers.
Experience gained by actually doing a job, not just studying it.
The natural features of an area of land as you see it.
The manner in which a person or group lives.
Harmful substances that make the air dirty.
Knowledge of a subject that you already have beforehand.
Having a strong effect on the final result; crucial.
Made by people, not occurring in nature.
Able to make people believe or agree.
Coming or existing in the time ahead.
A touch with the lips to show love or greeting.
A bending of the body forward to show respect.
To hold one another closely in the arms.
To bend the upper body forward as a sign of respect.
A change that fixes a mistake.
The particular way in which someone writes.
The space between two points; also emotional coolness.
Something that is not allowed to be done or spoken about.
A person who is in charge of others at work.
A person who is employed to work for someone.
A place or post where someone is employed.
A device worn over the ears to listen privately.
A device that turns electrical signals into sound.
Devices that work using electric circuits and chips.
A company that makes goods, especially in factories.
Regular care done to keep a machine working well.
The particular choice of words used to say something.
The letters or emails exchanged between people.
The quality of being short in time or length.
To gain the skills or right to do something.
Relating to one particular person; separate and personal.
A length of time with a start and an end.
The broken remains of destroyed buildings.
The 1989 opening of the Berlin Wall.
To place a crown on someone as ruler; also to complete gloriously.
A building where Muslims gather to pray.
